evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` remains a high-fan-in compatibility hub (129 importers in packet context) and still exports deprecated runtime access (`getLegacySupabaseClient`) plus policy constants, concentrating migration pressure into one cross-cutting alias.

`src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` derives `GenerationFilters` from `Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3]` imported from `@/shared/hooks/useProjectGenerations`, tightly coupling a feature-layer preloader to a shared hook function signature instead of a stable contract type.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts` both coordinate behavior via global/document event interception patterns, creating implicit cross-module interaction contracts (event cancellation, `[data-popup]` click filtering) rather than explicit shared interfaces.

`src/types/generationAndShots.ts` and `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` act as broad shared hubs; both are imported widely and aggregate unrelated concerns (domain type barrel + route/ID registry), increasing blast radius of local changes.

Task payload construction and payload reading are split across multiple files with duplicated field-level fallback logic (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`).

Lightbox/edit hooks build very large projected state objects and memo models repeatedly (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`).
Broad interfaces are being satisfied with explicit no-op callbacks (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`), indicating abstraction mismatch at call boundaries.

Rate-limit helper documents fail-open behavior and computes a fallback allowed result, but error paths return `operationFailure(...)` (`ok:false`) instead of an allowed value (`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts` lines 72-95, 141-159, 188-195).

Result contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts`) treats `ok:false` as failure regardless of policy, so fail-open semantics are encoded in `policy` while the primary success/failure discriminator says failure.

Travel request building exports both legacy and V2 constructors plus test-only public exports, while widening `motionMode` to `string` despite canonical motion mode being a strict union in task types (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts` lines 396-449, 572-589; `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` lines 218-223).
`src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` is a pure deprecated re-export shim with no owner/removal target metadata.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` and `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` use explicit deprecation contracts, but migration governance is inconsistent across shims.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` still accepts legacy path and legacy video-edit shapes and only partially gates legacy usage by removal target.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` retains deprecated payload shape contracts as active exported types.

Assigned files include three compatibility shims in broad shared roots: `src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` re-exports from `src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits`, `src/shared/components/AuthGate.tsx` re-exports from `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate`, and `src/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` re-exports from `src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer`.

Holistic structure shows flat-directory overload in both relevant roots: `src/shared/components/` has 70 files (55 production, 15 test) and `src/shared/hooks/` has 81 production files, both well above the >10 mixed-concern threshold.

Test placement is inconsistent across adjacent shared packages: hook tests are centralized (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` has 81 test files) while component tests are split between root files like `src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx` and a separate `src/shared/components/__tests__/` directory (34 imports into `src/shared/components/`).

Coupling confirms heavy shared-root gravity that makes placement ambiguity costly: `src/shared/components/ → src/shared/hooks/` (44 edges) and `src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/` (48 edges), so unclear entrypoints in these roots increase navigation and ownership friction.
package_organization.issues_preventing_higher_score: Shared root folders are overloaded and still contain migration-era proxy files plus mixed test-location conventions, which weakens directory boundaries and discoverability.

evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx` defines a very wide `MediaLightboxProps` surface (lines 11-77), then repackages it via `useBundledLightboxProps` (lines 79-147), while downstream hooks re-expand those values again.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` composes `UseSharedLightboxStateProps` by extending six concern-specific interfaces (lines 127-133) and returns a large nested facade (lines 144-253), indicating orchestration + state shaping + UI contract assembly are coupled in one module.

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts` and `.../formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts` forward long, near-identical parameter sets across wrappers (useFormSubmission lines 21-48, 73-91, 93-108; useSubmissionHandlers lines 8-23, 25-60) before actual submission logic runs.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` and `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-child.ts` both implement near-identical pair-id-then-child-order lookup strategies for child matching (individualTravelSegment lines 654-699; generation-child lines 184-223), duplicating a core workflow rule across frontend/server boundaries.

`useGetTask` in `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` fetches `tasks` by `id` only and does not take or enforce a `project_id`, while sibling mutation paths in `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` enforce project scope via `ensureProjectScope` + `verify*Scope`.

Auth posture differs across edge surfaces: `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` explicitly calls `authenticateRequest` and requires `auth.isServiceRole`, but completion logic modules (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`) expose privileged data-update functions with no explicit caller-auth or role invariant at module boundary.

findings.summary: Deprecated Supabase facade is still a subsystem dependency hub, keeping legacy boundary alive and widening blast radius.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is explicitly deprecated but still exports operational API (`getLegacySupabaseClient`) and migration policy symbols; packet context shows 129 importers.
Facade retains module-level mutable state (`hasWarnedPastRemovalTarget`) that changes process behavior across all call sites, signaling hidden shared runtime coupling.
Another high-fan-in shared runtime hub (`handleError.ts`, 244 importers in packet context) indicates the same centralization pattern across integration/runtime boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split migration metadata exports from runtime client access, freeze new imports with lint/path restrictions, and migrate call sites to runtime client entrypoints in staged slices so the legacy facade can become a thin compile-time alias, then be removed.

findings.summary: Feature preloader depends on another module’s function parameter shape, creating brittle cross-module coupling.
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` defines `type GenerationFilters = NonNullable<Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3]>`, binding feature contract to an external function’s argument index.
Any reorder/refactor of `fetchGenerations` parameters in `useProjectGenerations` silently changes this feature’s type contract without an explicit shared interface boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce and export a named `GenerationFilters` type from the generation-query contract module (or a domain types module), then consume that explicit type in the preloader instead of reflective `Parameters<>` indexing.

findings.summary: Gallery and lightbox modules coordinate through implicit global DOM event behavior instead of explicit interaction boundaries.
evidence: `LightboxShell.tsx` cancels dialog open-change events and manually preserves propagation, replacing library dismissal semantics with custom interaction orchestration.
`useMobileInteractions.ts` installs document/window listeners and closes UI based on `target.closest('[data-popup]')`, relying on structural DOM markers defined elsewhere.
Cross-component popup/action UI in `ShotActions.tsx` uses tooltip/select overlays that must align with those global rules, but there is no explicit shared interaction policy type/API.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared interaction boundary for overlay/popover lifecycle (single source of truth for outside-click, pointer-dismiss, and mobile tap rules) and route lightbox/gallery consumers through that abstraction instead of ad hoc global listeners/selectors.

findings.summary: High-fan-in shared hubs aggregate unrelated concerns, increasing coupling between independent modules.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is a canonical barrel with very high importer count (205 in packet context), so type-surface churn propagates broadly.

`toolConstants.ts` stores both IDs and route paths in shared space; `CreativePartnerPane.tsx` consumes tool navigation constants directly from shared, coupling page/pane content to tool routing internals.
These hubs are convenient but blur boundaries between domain types, routing policy, and UI modules.
findings.suggestion: Partition shared hubs by ownership boundary (e.g., `domain/types`, `routing/contracts`, `ui/primitives`) and expose narrowly scoped re-exports; keep marketing/home consumers on a stable app-routing contract instead of tool-specific constants.

findings.summary: Task payload fields are duplicated across builders/readers, forcing fallback probing and weakening interface honesty.
evidence: `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` writes `num_new_segments_to_generate` and many orchestrator fields manually (`payloadBuilder.ts`), while `resolveExpectedSegmentCount` must read both `orchestrator_details.num_new_segments_to_generate` and top-level `num_new_segments_to_generate` (`orchestrator.ts:240-245`).

`individualTravelSegment.ts` emits overlapping data in multiple locations (e.g., `orchestrator_details`, top-level pair fields, and `individual_segment_params` around lines 493, 499, 526, 618), which increases drift risk when adding/changing fields.
Join-clips follows a separate manual payload composition path (`joinClips.ts`) instead of a shared codec, continuing the same per-family duplication pattern.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one typed payload codec per task family (encode/decode + defaults) and make builders/readers consume that single contract; remove reader-side fallback probing once canonical field placement is enforced.

findings.summary: Edit/lightbox hooks repeatedly project the same state into multiple large objects, creating high indirection cost and update drift.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` constructs `imageEditValue` plus separate `inpaintingState`/`transformState`/`generationState` sections with overlapping members (notably around lines 344-492).

`ImageLightbox.tsx` and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` both build large memoized view-model bundles (`lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, controls-panel payloads), duplicating projection logic for image vs video paths.
Large projection objects require very long dependency lists, increasing maintenance overhead and chance of stale or inconsistent wiring during feature changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared lightbox/edit state adapter layer that exposes smaller domain-specific selectors (variants/media/navigation/edit) and compose UI props from those selectors near the consuming components.

findings.summary: No-op callbacks are passed to satisfy broad mode APIs, signaling that the interface contract does not match actual mode capabilities.
evidence: `ImageLightbox.tsx` passes `handleEnterMagicEditMode: () => {}` (line ~275) and `handleEnterVideoEditMode: () => {}` (line ~376) in different setup paths.
These placeholders exist because shared orchestration/layout contracts include handlers that are irrelevant in the current mode, but still mandatory at call sites.
This pattern hides invalid states behind silent no-ops instead of making impossible states unrepresentable at the type level.
findings.suggestion: Refactor mode contracts into discriminated unions (image-edit vs video-edit) so handlers are required only in valid modes; remove no-op fillers and branch on mode-specific interfaces.

findings.summary: Fail-open rate-limit paths still return `ok:false`, conflicting with allow-request semantics.
evidence: `checkRateLimit` logs "allowing request" and computes `fallback = failOpenRateLimit(...)`, but returns `operationFailure(...)` for RPC/runtime/payload issues (`rateLimit.ts` lines 141-159, 188-195).
In the shared result model, `ok:false` is the canonical failure branch (`edgeOperation.ts` lines 4-24, 50-67), so callers that branch on `ok` will treat fail-open conditions as hard failures.
This creates two parallel meanings of failure (`ok:false` vs `policy:'fail_open'`), increasing caller inconsistency risk at API boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Make fail-open paths return `operationSuccess(fallback, { policy: 'fail_open' })` (or a dedicated degraded-success variant) and reserve `ok:false` for true deny/error paths; update shared helpers so callers can branch consistently on one discriminator.

findings.summary: Travel request builders expose overlapping legacy/V2 APIs and widen typed motion mode to `string`.
evidence: `generateVideoService` defines both `TravelRequestBodyV2Params` and `TravelRequestBodyLegacyParams`, plus exports `_buildTravelRequestBody` and `_buildTravelRequestBodyV2` (lines 420-449, 572-589), creating multiple external construction surfaces for the same payload.

`MotionParams.motionMode` and legacy `motionMode` are typed as `string` (lines 396-399, 446), while canonical task API expects `motion_mode?: 'basic' | 'presets' | 'advanced'` (`types.ts` lines 141-146, 218-223).
The widened internal API permits invalid modes to flow into `buildMotionTaskFields(...)`, weakening contract predictability at the request boundary.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one public request-construction path (V2), keep legacy adapter private/internal, and narrow `motionMode` types to `MotionConfig['motion_mode']` (or shared union alias) across builder params so invalid modes are rejected at compile time.

findings.summary: Compatibility shims follow inconsistent sunset governance, so legacy surfaces can persist without a uniform removal mechanism.
evidence: `useCredits.ts` has only a deprecation comment and re-export, with no owner/removal target contract.
`handleError.ts` and `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` include explicit `@deprecationOwner`/`@removalTarget` metadata and policy hooks, showing a different governance style from other shims.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` uses policy-backed owner/remove-by exports, but remains an active compatibility contract.
findings.suggestion: Define one deprecation contract standard (owner, remove-by, enforcement path, telemetry) and apply it to every compatibility surface, including thin re-export shims like `useCredits`.

findings.summary: Join-clips compatibility enforcement is asymmetric: legacy clip-path fields are sunset-gated, but legacy video-edit fields are still open-ended.
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` enforces remove-by only for legacy clip-path mode via `assertLegacyClipSourceAllowed` + `isPastRemovalTarget`.
The same adapter still accepts `video_edit_mode` + `source_*` payload fields in `resolveVideoEditConfig` without equivalent post-target rejection.
Error messaging still treats legacy video-edit fields as first-class migration fallback (`video_edit ... or legacy video_edit_mode/source_* fields`).
findings.suggestion: Add the same remove-by gate for legacy video-edit payload fields and emit structured migration telemetry whenever any legacy path is used.

findings.summary: Field-normalization fallback chains are duplicated across utilities, which increases branch complexity and drift risk.
evidence: `galleryUtils.ts` resolves explicit vs orchestrator array fields with custom precedence and inline coercion.
`mediaTypeHelpers.ts` separately resolves `generation_id` from multiple candidate fields (`generation_id`, `metadata.generation_id`, `id`).
`taskIdHelpers.ts` independently resolves task IDs via `source_task_id || orchestrator_task_id || task_id` with local UUID validation.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared normalization layer for legacy/current generation/task param shapes and reuse it in these utilities to remove repeated precedence logic.

findings.summary: ID/filename uniqueness generation uses inconsistent primitives and formatting conventions across nearby utilities.
evidence: `storagePaths.ts` uses `Math.random().toString(36)` for filename entropy in both `generateUniqueFilename` and `generateThumbnailFilename`.
`taskCreation/ids.ts` uses `crypto.randomUUID` with `nanoid` fallback and then truncates UUID entropy for task IDs.
Both modules implement separate timestamp formatting logic instead of a shared utility.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one entropy source and one timestamp/identifier formatter (shared utility) for storage names and task/run IDs.

findings.summary: Migration proxy files remain in overloaded shared roots, obscuring real ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useCredits.ts` is a deprecated compatibility shim re-exporting from `@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits`.
`AuthGate.tsx` and `AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` are thin re-export facades pointing to deeper auth and generation-controls packages.
These shims sit in already overloaded roots (`src/shared/hooks/` 81 files, `src/shared/components/` 70 files), increasing ambiguity about canonical import paths.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create explicit compatibility zones `src/shared/compat/hooks/` and `src/shared/compat/components/`; (2) move shim files there in order `useCredits.ts`, `AuthGate.tsx`, `AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx`; (3) update imports to canonical owners (`@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits`, `@/shared/auth/components/AuthGate`, `@/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer`) and keep temporary compat re-exports only in the new compat folder; (4) validate with `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run`, then `desloppify move <src> <dst>`, `rg "@/shared/(hooks/useCredits|components/AuthGate|components/AspectRatioVisualizer)" src`, `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, and `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx`.

findings.summary: Shared tests use mixed placement conventions across adjacent packages, reducing navigability.
evidence: Hook tests in this batch live under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/`, matching a centralized test directory pattern.
`BulkEditControls.test.tsx` is in `src/shared/components/` root while the repo also has `src/shared/components/__tests__/` for many component tests.
Directory profile shows `src/shared/components/` mixes 55 production and 15 test files, indicating test artifacts are not consistently separated in that package.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) pick one convention for `src/shared/components` (recommended: centralize to `src/shared/components/__tests__/`); (2) move root-level component tests first for low-risk files such as `BulkEditControls.test.tsx`; (3) enforce path conventions for new tests; (4) update relative imports after each move and verify with `desloppify move src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx --dry-run`, then apply move, run `rg "BulkEditControls.test" src/shared/components`, `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx`, and `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase compatibility facade remains a core dependency instead of an edge shim.
evidence: README positions `@/integrations/supabase/legacy` as transitional with removal target and governance checks.

`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` still exports live runtime accessor (`getLegacySupabaseClient`) and packet metrics show 129 importers, including broad usage from shared hooks/components, which means migration boundary has not contracted.
findings.suggestion: Enforce architectural quarantine for legacy Supabase access: (1) create `src/integrations/supabase/runtime/publicClient.ts` as the only supported app-facing accessor, (2) migrate high-volume importers from `@/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade` to runtime accessor in waves (`src/shared/hooks/` -> `src/shared/components/` -> `src/tools/**`), (3) demote legacy facade to throwing stub behind explicit env-guard after budget reaches zero, and (4) keep gate commands in CI (`npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run quality:check`) to validate no regressions.

findings.summary: UI primitive button depends on theme package, inverting expected layer direction.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `APP_BUTTON_SIZES` and `APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS` from `src/shared/components/theme/buttonVariants` while serving as shared primitive (`@uiContract`).
This couples foundational UI package behavior to a higher-level theme module, making primitive contracts contingent on app-layer configuration.
findings.suggestion: Move extensibility seam to explicit composition boundary: keep base variants inside `ui/button.tsx`, then apply app-specific variants in a wrapper like `src/shared/components/theme/AppButton.tsx` that composes `Button` rather than mutating primitive variant map via import-time merge.

findings.summary: Generation type contracts are split between `src/types` and `src/domains/generation/types`.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is the canonical barrel but re-exports legacy `@/types/*` files.

Packet structure shows a second contract package at `src/domains/generation/types/` with overlapping filenames (`generationParams.ts`, `generationMetadata.ts`, `generationRow.ts`, `shot.ts`) and much lower adoption, indicating unresolved package migration.
findings.suggestion: Run staged reorg to converge ownership: Stage 1 target folder `src/domains/generation/contracts/` (move domain type files there, keep temporary re-export shims in `src/types/`); Stage 2 move barrel to `src/domains/generation/contracts/index.ts` and repoint imports by area (`src/shared/hooks/**` then `src/shared/components/**` then `src/tools/**`); Stage 3 delete temporary shims once zero imports remain. Validate each stage with `rg "@/types/(generation|shot|generationAndShots)" src`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: The `shared/components/ui` package is overloaded and too flat for its concern breadth.
evidence: Holistic structure reports `src/shared/components/ui/` has 58 files with mixed primitives, helpers, hooks, and runtime registration artifacts.
Assigned files (`button.tsx`, `cn.ts`) sit beside policy/runtime helpers in the same top-level folder, obscuring ownership and increasing navigation cost.
findings.suggestion: Use staged folder reorg inside `src/shared/components/ui/`: Stage 1 create `primitives/`, `styles/`, `runtime/`, `hooks/`; Stage 2 move low-risk utilities first (`cn.ts` -> `styles/cn.ts`, `button.tsx` -> `primitives/button.tsx`) and add index re-exports; Stage 3 move runtime/hook files and then update direct imports with `rg "@/shared/components/ui/" src --glob '!**/*.test.*'` plus codemod/search-replace. Validate with `npm run typecheck`, `npm test -- src/shared/components/ui`, and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Lightbox flow relies on oversized prop/facade contracts that add indirection without reducing coupling
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` carries many unrelated concerns in one interface (navigation, shot ops, edits, details, segment mode, trim mode) at lines 11-77, then `useBundledLightboxProps` groups them (79-147) and passes them onward.

`useSharedLightboxState` accepts a mega-prop contract (`UseSharedLightboxStateProps` extends 6 interfaces at 127-133) and returns another large nested contract (144-253, 688-734), so callers must understand both the input facade and output facade.
`ImageLightbox` still reconstructs and forwards many individual fields into `useSharedLightboxState` (228-260), so the grouping layer does not materially reduce call-site coupling.
findings.suggestion: Split the lightbox boundary into explicit domain controllers (`useLightboxVariantsController`, `useLightboxShotController`, `useLightboxLayoutController`) with narrow input contracts, and pass a single typed context object per controller instead of flattening/rebundling the same fields through `MediaLightbox -> Image/Video -> shared hook`.

findings.summary: Form submission logic is spread across wrapper layers that mainly relay state, obscuring ownership
evidence: `useFormSubmission` snapshots many fields into `formStateRef` (51-70), then passes a similar field set to `useTaskParamsBuilder` (73-91), then forwards another overlapping set to `useSubmissionHandlers` (93-108).
`useSubmissionHandlers` defines another wide props interface (8-23) and largely re-forwards values into `useSubmissionOrchestrator` (45-60), creating a relay chain before executing behavior.

`useSubmissionOrchestrator` contains four queueing commands (`submitManaged`, `submitAutomated`, `queueExisting`, `queueLikeExisting`) with repeated `queueIncomingTask` orchestration structure (91-100, 129-174, 193-202, 224-256), mixing validation, prompt transformation, and scheduling in one hook.
findings.suggestion: Collapse the wrapper chain into a single command-layer hook that receives one immutable `SubmissionContext` object plus strategy functions; then implement each submit mode as data-driven command specs (label, preconditions, promptSource, taskParamBuilder) to remove repeated queue scaffolding.

findings.summary: Child-generation matching rules are duplicated in two modules, risking drift in core behavior
evidence: `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask` performs two-stage child lookup: first `pair_shot_generation_id`, then fallback to `child_order` only when pair id is absent (654-699), with behavioral comments describing why fallback is gated.
`findExistingGenerationAtPosition` in edge function repeats the same two-stage policy and same fallback gating semantics (184-223), including comments about not falling back when pair id exists.
Both locations encode policy logic independently, so any future change to matching semantics must be updated twice across client/server codepaths.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical child-matching policy contract in shared task metadata (e.g., `child_match_strategy` + explicit fallback flags) and centralize the resolver in one runtime boundary; the other side should consume result/contract rather than re-implementing the decision tree.

findings.summary: Single-task read path skips project-scope guard used by sibling data mutations
evidence: `useGetTask(taskId)` queries `.from('tasks').select('*').eq('id', taskId).single()` with no `project_id` constraint and no preflight scope verifier.

In the same subsystem, mutation paths (`updateGenerationLocation`, `toggleGenerationStar`, delete helpers) require `projectId` and call `ensureProjectScope` plus `verifyGenerationScope`/`verifyVariantScope` before write operations.
findings.suggestion: Make task reads follow the same scope contract: require `projectId` in `useGetTask`, filter by both `id` and `project_id`, and/or introduce a shared `verifyTaskScope` helper used by all task read/write hooks.

findings.summary: Privileged completion modules rely on implicit caller trust instead of explicit auth invariant
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` enforces `authenticateRequest(...)` and rejects non-service-role callers (`service_role_required`).

`complete_task` orchestration/generation modules perform direct `tasks` updates and generation routing but have no explicit role/actor assertion at their boundary; they depend on upstream caller behavior rather than a local invariant.
findings.suggestion: Standardize privileged-edge authorization by passing a validated auth context (e.g., `isServiceRole: true`) into completion functions and asserting it in shared entry wrappers, so all sensitive edge flows fail closed if wired from an unauthenticated/incorrect handler.
